rankings.name: OpenAI (Operator + Codex)
highlights: Operator agent expanding to more countries
Codex code agent gaining enterprise traction
Deep research agent now integrated in ChatGPT Pro

rankings.name: Anthropic (Claude Agent SDK)
highlights: Claude Agent SDK adoption accelerating in enterprise
Computer use and tool-use benchmarks leading industry
Model Context Protocol becoming de facto standard

rankings.name: Google DeepMind (Gemini Agents)
highlights: Project Mariner browser agent in limited preview
Gemini 2.5 Pro agentic capabilities strong
Google Agentspace for enterprise gaining momentum

rankings.name: Microsoft (Copilot Studio + AutoGen)
highlights: Copilot Studio autonomous agents in GA
AutoGen v0.4 multi-agent framework maturing
Azure AI Agent Service expanding capabilities

rankings.name: CrewAI
highlights: Enterprise tier gaining Fortune 500 clients
CrewAI Flows simplifying complex orchestration
Growing open-source community momentum

rankings.name: LangChain (LangGraph)
highlights: LangGraph Studio for visual agent debugging
LangSmith observability improving
Competition from simpler frameworks increasing

rankings.name: Salesforce (Agentforce)
highlights: Agentforce 2.0 with multi-agent orchestration
CRM-native agents differentiating from generic platforms
Strong enterprise pipeline

rankings.name: Amazon (Bedrock Agents)
highlights: Multi-agent collaboration feature released
AWS enterprise install base advantage
Nova models improving agent task completion

rankings.name: Mistral (Le Chat Agents)
highlights: Le Chat with canvas and web search
European sovereignty positioning strong
Agent capabilities still behind US leaders

rankings.name: Hugging Face (smolagents)
highlights: smolagents framework lightweight and popular
Open-source agent ecosystem growing
Community-driven approach differentiating

trend: The agent builder war is consolidating around two models: platform-native (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google building agents INTO their products) vs. framework-agnostic (CrewAI, LangGraph letting you build ON TOP of any model). Week 12 shows platform-native pulling ahead on capabilities while framework-agnostic players compete on flexibility and price.
